refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall derive performance requirements from the worksite mission context.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined multiple obligations: deriving, recording, and managing performance requirements. To ensure atomicity and solution neutrality, these were separated into distinct sub-FRs. Additionally, the FR focuses on what the system must do (derive requirements) without specifying how (method).
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can identify and specify performance requirements based solely on the worksite mission context without reference to implementation details.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall derive performance requirements from the worksite mission context.
The NGHE solution shall record performance requirements derived from the worksite mission context.
The NGHE solution shall manage performance requirements derived from the worksite mission context.

candidate_dps: Define a logical model to translate worksite mission context into performance requirements
Establish a traceable mapping framework linking worksite inputs to performance targets
Implement a version-controlled repository to maintain history of baselines and calibration updates
Develop a policy compliance module to incorporate safety plans and operational policies into performance derivation
selected_dp.selected_dp: Establish a traceable mapping framework linking worksite inputs to performance targets

potential_sources: Tight integration between mapping framework and policy compliance module could cause coupling if not modularized
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on a logical mapping framework that can be modularized separately from policy compliance and history management, supporting independence.

assumptions_risks: Assumes worksite inputs are well-defined and consistently formatted
Risk that changes in operational policies may require updates to mapping framework
Maintaining traceability may be complex if inputs or requirements evolve rapidly
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly separating policy compliance and history management into distinct DPs or IMs to reduce coupling and improve modularity.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for integrating and managing data flows and performance targets across the system

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives worksite input data and system status information

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends performance targets and task assignments based on mapping framework

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as the mapping framework logically belongs to the central solution managing data and performance targets; minimal diffusion as interfaces are well defined here.
Partial containment possible but mapping framework would require significant data exchange with other modules, causing diffusion.
Low containment; teleoperation focuses on remote control rather than data mapping; would cause high diffusion.
Very low containment; safety services are peripheral to mapping framework; high diffusion expected.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for the DP as it centralizes data integration and performance target management, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data from external sources.
Risk of overloading NGHE Solution with too many responsibilities if other DPs are also assigned here.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP clearly addresses the FR by establishing a traceable mapping framework that links worksite inputs to performance targets, fulfilling the requirement to derive performance requirements from the mission context.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data from external sources.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high containment (score 8) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating that the DP is well localized within this IM boundary. Interfaces are well defined, minimizing diffusion risk.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the NGHE Solution module receives telemetry inputs and outputs performance targets, enabling traceability and documentation of performance targets as per acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear with the NGHE Solution module responsible for integrating data flows and managing performance targets, aligning well with the FR and DP.

recommended_revisions: Monitor the risk of overloading the NGHE Solution module with multiple responsibilities to avoid architectural complexity.
Ensure robust handling and validation of telemetry data to mitigate dependency risks.
Document interface contracts explicitly to maintain clarity and reduce integration issues.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current level of decomposition sufficiently addresses the functional requirements with clear mappings and feasible verification. The NGHE Solution module demonstrates high containment and low diffusion risk, and the allocation of responsibilities is clear. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to avoid unnecessary further decomposition that could introduce complexity and risk. Instead, focus should be on monitoring identified risks and ensuring robust implementation and documentation as recommended.

revise.why: To address the recommended revisions, particularly monitoring module responsibility overload and ensuring robust telemetry data handling, architectural adjustments or clarifications at the implementation module level may be needed.

assumptions: Telemetry data will be timely and accurate as required.
The NGHE Solution module can handle the current scope without performance degradation.
open_risks: Dependency on external telemetry data accuracy and timeliness.
Potential architectural complexity if NGHE Solution module responsibilities increase.
evidence_needed: Verification results demonstrating traceability and performance target accuracy.
Documentation of interface contracts and telemetry data validation procedures.
